title: PHP 8.5: "Using null as an array offset" deprecation — ModelSynth/EloquentCollectionSynth pass null into Relation::getMorphedModel()
author: losnyr
body:
### Summary
On PHP 8.5, every request (and every test) that hydrates a Livewire component holding an Eloquent **model** or **collection** property emits:

```
Deprecated: Using null as an array offset is deprecated, use an empty string instead
in .../laravel/framework/src/Illuminate/Database/Eloquent/Relations/Relation.php on line 511
```

(`Relation::getMorphedModel()` does `return static::$morphMap[$alias] ?? null;`.) It's harmless functionally, but extremely high-volume — a single test run of our app produces ~560 of these lines, drowning CI logs.

### Root cause
Livewire hydration passes a possibly-null class straight into `Relation::getMorphedModel()` without guarding:

`src/Features/SupportModels/ModelSynth.php` (`hydrate`):
```php
$class = $meta['class'] ?? null;
$aliasClass = Relation::getMorphedModel($class);   // $class can be null
```

`src/Features/SupportModels/EloquentCollectionSynth.php` (`hydrate`):
```php
$modelClass = $meta['modelClass'];                 // null for an empty EloquentCollection
$modelAlias = Relation::getMorphedModel($modelClass);
```
(`dehydrate()` stores `modelClass => null` when `getQueueableClass()` returns null, i.e. an empty collection.)

On PHP 8.5, `$morphMap[null]` triggers the deprecation. `getMorphedModel(null)` returns `null` anyway, so guarding the call is behavior-preserving.

### Environment
- livewire/livewire v4.3.3 (also reproduces on `main` as of 2026-07-10 — unchanged)
- laravel/framework v13.18.1 (also unguarded on latest v13.19.0)
- PHP 8.5.4

### Suggested fix
Guard the null before the call in both synths, e.g.:
```php
$aliasClass = $class === null ? null : Relation::getMorphedModel($class);
```
(Alternatively, Laravel could null-guard inside `getMorphedModel()`, but the null originates here.)

### Minimal repro
A Livewire component with a `public ?Model $model` (or a `public Collection $items` that's an empty `EloquentCollection`), rendered on PHP 8.5, emits the deprecation on the second (hydration) request.

title: [4.x] Livewire::test() silently absorbs TypeErrors thrown from component actions
author: calebporzio
body:
While digging into #10301 I noticed something about the v4 test harness that made that bug harder to diagnose than it should have been.

If a component action throws a `TypeError` (for example the `array_merge(): Argument #1 must be of type array, Illuminate\Support\Collection given` from #10301 when `messages()` returns a Collection), calling it through the test harness swallows it:

```php
Livewire::test(MyComponent::class)
    ->call('save')      // TypeError thrown inside — absorbed
    ->assertHasErrors(); // fails "cleanly" — no sign an exception ever happened
```

No exception surfaces, `$component->getErrorBag()` is empty, and nothing hints that the action blew up. The same request over real HTTP is a 500 with a stack trace — which is why reporters keep pasting production stack traces we can't reproduce "in tests".

Worth discussing whether `call()` should rethrow non-validation throwables (validation exceptions obviously need to keep being captured for `assertHasErrors()`). Some existing regression tests rely on the current absorb-and-assert pattern, so this would need a careful pass rather than a blanket rethrow.

Repro details are in #10301's thread.

title: BaseRenderless and BaseAuthorize attribute doesnt work inside browser test when action trigerred as event listener
author: ghabriel25
body:
### Livewire version v4.3 ### Laravel version v13 ### PHP version 8.4 ### Browser and operating system N/A ### Describe the issue you're experiencing When using `#[BaseRenderless]` or `#[BaseAuthorize]` inside browser test, triggering an action as event listener doesnt work. Its because inside `SupportEvents::call()` filter the attribute as a subclass not instance ```php $this->component->getAttributes() ->filter(fn ($i) => is_subclass_of($i, BaseAuthorize::class)) ->filter(fn ($i) => $i->getName() === $method) ->filter(fn ($i) => $i->getLevel() === AttributeLevel::METHOD) ->each(fn ($i) => $i->call($params)); ``` ### Suggestion ```php $this->component->getAttributes() ->filter(fn ($i) => $i instanceof BaseAuthorize::class) ->filter(fn ($i) => $i->getName() === $method) ->filter(fn ($i) => $i->getLevel() === AttributeLevel::METHOD) ->each(fn ($i) => $i->call($params)); ``` ### Code snippets to reproduce the issue ```php public functio test_can_trigger_renderless_attribute_with_event() { Livewire::visit(new class extends Component { public $count = 0; #[BaseRenderless] #[BaseOn('some-event') function renderlessAction() { } function render() { $this->count++; return Blade::render(<<<'HTML'
HTML, ['count' => $this->count]); } }) ->assertSeeIn('@button', '1') ->waitForLivewire()->click('@button') ->assertSeeIn('@button', '1'); } ``` ### Screenshots/screen recordings N/A ### How do you expect it to work? When user wants to contribute and creating BrowserTest, this could lead to unusual behavior. I also notice some of unit test also using `Base**` attribute. ### Please confirm (incomplete submissions will not be addressed) - [x] I have provided easy and step-by-step instructions to reproduce the bug. - [x] I have provided code samples as text and NOT images. - [x] I understand my bug report will be removed if I haven't met the criteria above.

title: Dynamic `x-mask` broken when used together with `wire:model`
author: CasEbb
body:
### Livewire version

v4.3.3

### Laravel version

v13.18.0

### PHP version

8.5.7

### Browser and operating system

Chrome on macOS

### Describe the issue you're experiencing

When dynamically determining `x-mask` for a text input, the mask fails to work properly when the field also has `wire:model` and `x-mask` changes inbetween renders.

Considering the example below:

1. Starting with country 'FR' and postal code '12345' in a field masked '99999'
2. Change the country to 'BE' and the postal code changes to '1234' adhering to the mask '9999'. The mask still works fine.
3. Change the country back to 'FR' (check your web developer console to ensure that `x-mask` has been updated again) and type a number in the input. It won't show up. If you type the same number again, it will show up. If you type another number, the input is still limited to 4 numbers (instead of the expected 5).

- This behaviour doesn't occur if the field doesn't have `wire:model`.
- Bissecting shows this bug was introduced between v4.2.2 and v4.2.3 (presumably the Alpine update from v3.15.8 to v3.15.9)

### Code snippets to reproduce the issue

```php
<?php

use Livewire\Attributes\Computed;
use Livewire\Component;

new class extends Component {
    public string $country = 'FR';
    public string $postalCode = '12345';

    #[Computed]
    public function mask()
    {
        return $this->country === 'FR' ? '99999' : '9999';
    }
};

?>
```
```blade
<form wire:submit="save">
    <select wire:model.live="country">
        <option value="FR">France</option>
        <option value="BE">Belgium</option>
    </select>
    <input wire:model="postalCode" x-mask="{{ $this->mask }}">
    <button type="submit">Save</button>
</form>
```

### Screenshots/screen recordings

_No response_

### How do you expect it to work?

`x-mask` is supposed to work properly after changing between renders

### Please confirm (incomplete submissions will not be addressed)

- [x] I have provided easy and step-by-step instructions to reproduce the bug.
- [x] I have provided code samples as text and NOT images.
- [x] I understand my bug report will be removed if I haven't met the criteria above.

title: Livewire continues processing redirected HTML response after handling fetch redirect
author: jancinert
body:
### What happened?

When a Livewire update request receives an HTTP redirect (for example because an auth/session middleware redirects to `/login`), the browser `fetch()` follows the redirect and Livewire receives the final HTML response.

Livewire detects `response.redirected === true` and invokes the redirect handler, which eventually calls `window.location.href = response.url`. However, the request flow continues processing the already-read response body after that point. Because the body is usually an HTML login page rather than a Livewire JSON payload, Livewire can still try to parse/process the redirected HTML before the browser completes the top-level navigation.

In practice this can cause a brief flash of redirected, unstyled HTML or an error fallback/modal before the navigation to the redirected URL completes.

### Reproduction

1. Have a Livewire component with any action that sends a normal Livewire update request.
2. Make the server respond to that update request with a normal HTTP redirect, such as `302 Location: /login` from an expired session/auth middleware.
3. Trigger the Livewire action after the session expires.
4. The update request is redirected to `/login`, so the final fetch response body is the login page HTML.
5. Livewire calls the redirect handler, but then continues to parse/process that HTML response as if it were the Livewire payload.

### Expected behavior

After Livewire handles a non-prevented `response.redirected` redirect, it should stop processing the response body for that request.

If a userland redirect hook calls `preventDefault()`, then continuing the current flow may still make sense. But when the default redirect behavior actually runs, the request should return early instead of attempting to parse the redirected HTML as JSON.

### Possible fix direction

Have the redirect handler report whether it actually performed the redirect, then return early in the request flow only for non-prevented redirects. Roughly:

```js
if (response.redirected) {
    let handled = handlers.redirect(response.url)

    if (handled) {
        handlers.finish()
        return
    }
}
```

and make the redirect handler return `false` when `preventDefault()` was called, otherwise call `window.location.href = url` and return `true`.

### Why this matters

Applications can work around this by returning JSON/401 for Livewire requests instead of `302`, but a regular Laravel auth/session redirect is a common failure mode. Once Livewire has already decided to perform a top-level redirect, continuing to process the redirected HTML body is surprising and can briefly leak an unstyled intermediate page to users.

title: #[Validate()] and rules() arrays are incorrectly merged
author: theticraft

title: Support namespaced translation keys in pagination views
author: erickcomp
body:
### Livewire version

v4.3.1

### Laravel version

v13.8

### PHP version

8.4.22

### Browser and operating system

Firefox on OpenSUSE Tumbleweed

### Describe the issue you're experiencing

## Problem

The pagination views currently use global JSON translation strings:
```blade 
{{ __('Showing') }}
{{ __('to') }}
{{ __('of') }}
{{ __('results') }}
```
This works well for applications because developers can simply add these strings to their *.json translation files.

However, it makes it difficult for package authors to provide localized experiences. Packages can easily ship namespaced translations via:

```php
$this->loadTranslationsFrom(...);
```

but they cannot provide translations for these global strings without:

Publishing JSON files into the host application's lang directory, or
Forking/copying the pagination views and maintaining them forever.

Both approaches have drawbacks.

## Proposed solution

Consider using namespaced translation keys in the pagination views, for example:

```blade 
{{ __('livewire::pagination.showing') }}
{{ __('livewire::pagination.to') }}
{{ __('livewire::pagination.of') }}
{{ __('livewire::pagination.results') }}
```

## Backward compatibility

I understand this would be a breaking change because existing applications may already rely on global JSON translations.

For that reason, this could be:

Scheduled for Livewire 5, or
Introduced with a fallback mechanism that first checks the namespaced key and then falls back to the current global string.

I think that something like this could work for the fallback mechanism:
```php
__('livewire::pagination.showing') !== 'livewire::pagination.showing'
    ? __('livewire::pagination.showing')
    : __('Showing') 
```

## Why this matters

Using namespaced keys would allow package authors to provide translations in a fully encapsulated way, without publishing global JSON files or maintaining copies of Livewire's pagination views.

## Real case scenario
I ran into this while developing a reusable Livewire table package that ships its own translations. The pagination strings are currently the only translations that cannot be encapsulated inside the package.

Thank you for considering it.

### Code snippets to reproduce the issue

Any pagination view cannot have its translations overriden in packages

### Screenshots/screen recordings

_No response_

### How do you expect it to work?

I expect it to have livewire namespaced translations.

### Please confirm (incomplete submissions will not be addressed)

- [x] I have provided easy and step-by-step instructions to reproduce the bug.
- [x] I have provided code samples as text and NOT images.
- [x] I understand my bug report will be removed if I haven't met the criteria above.

title: wire:sort with wire:sort:group: TypeError on cross-group drag in empty group
author: akamasAT
body:
### Livewire version v4.2.4 ### Laravel version v12.56 ### PHP version 8.4.16 ### Browser and operating system Chromium Win ### Describe the issue you're experiencing When using wire:sort with wire:sort:group to drag items between groups, a JavaScript error occurs in livewire.js when dropping an item into a different empty group: TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'before') at Block.appendChild (livewire.js:13659:23) at context.patchChildren (livewire.js:13490:20) ... This error breaks further Livewire execution on the page. The server-side callback runs correctly and the database is updated as expected — the issue is purely in the client-side DOM morphing after the update. Root cause wire:sort internally calls skipRender() after the sort callback to avoid unnecessary re-renders when reordering within the same group. However, when an item is dragged across groups (wire:sort:group), a full re-render is required because two separate DOM containers change. Livewire does not distinguish between these two cases and suppresses the re-render unconditionally, causing the DOM morphing algorithm to operate on a stale/inconsistent DOM state. To reproduce Set up a Livewire component with nested wire:sort and wire:sort:group Have at least two groups with items Drag an item from one group into an empty group Observe the TypeError in the browser console ### Code snippets to reproduce the issue ```
@forelse ($offerOptions as $option)
{{ $option->name }} || {{ $option->id }}
@if ($option->offeredProducts->count() > 0) @foreach ($option->offeredProducts as $offeredProduct)
{{ $offeredProduct->offer_option_order }} || {{ $offeredProduct->id }}
@endforeach @endif
@empty @endforelse
``` ### Screenshots/screen recordings _No response_ ### How do you expect it to work? The item is moved to the target group and the DOM updates correctly without any JavaScript errors. ### Please confirm (incomplete submissions will not be addressed) - [x] I have provided easy and step-by-step instructions to reproduce the bug. - [x] I have provided code samples as text and NOT images. - [x] I understand my bug report will be removed if I haven't met the criteria above.

title: ComponentNotFoundException during Checksum::verify() causes unhandled 500 instead of 400 (Livewire 4.x)
author: fleckiboy
body:
### Livewire version

4.3.0

### Laravel version

13.11.1

### PHP version

8.3.1

### Browser and operating system

Firefox on Windows

### Describe the issue you're experiencing

### Description
When a bot or malicious client sends a POST request to the Livewire update endpoint with a valid session and CSRF token, but with a snapshot referencing a non-existent component (e.g. from a Filament installation on a different server), Livewire throws an unhandled ComponentNotFoundException resulting in HTTP 500 instead of a graceful 400.

This is related to Discussion #9819 but is a **distinct issue:** #9819 covers malformed updates payloads causing TypeError. This issue covers a **valid, well-formed snapshot** referencing a **non-existent component name**, which causes ComponentNotFoundException to be thrown inside Checksum::verify() before the checksum validation is complete.

### Root cause
resolveComponentClass() is called inside Checksum::verify() via ReleaseToken::verify(), before it is established whether the payload is valid. If the component does not exist, the exception propagates unhandled up the stack.
The call order is:
```
HandleComponents->fromSnapshot()
  → Checksum::verify()
    → ReleaseToken::verify()
      → Factory->resolveComponentClass()   ← ComponentNotFoundException thrown here
``` 
A non-existent component name should be caught and treated as a corrupt/invalid payload rather than propagating as an unhandled exception.

### Actual behavior
HTTP 500 with an unhandled ComponentNotFoundException.
### Workaround
Catching the exception manually in app/Exceptions/Handler.php:
```php
use Livewire\Exceptions\ComponentNotFoundException;

public function register(): void
{
    $this->renderable(function (ComponentNotFoundException $e, $request) {
        if ($request->hasHeader('X-Livewire')) {
            \Log::warning('Livewire ComponentNotFoundException blocked', [
                'component' => $e->getMessage(),
                'ip' => $request->ip(),
            ]);
            return response()->json(['message' => 'Bad Request'], 400);
        }
    });
}
``` 
### Suggested fix
In ReleaseToken::verify() or Checksum::verify(), catch ComponentNotFoundException and treat it as a checksum failure – i.e. throw CorruptComponentPayloadException or return HTTP 400.
### Note on relation to #9819
Discussion #9819 and PR #9970 address malformed updates payloads. This issue addresses a different code path: a well-formed snapshot with a non-existent component name, which fails inside Checksum::verify() rather than during property hydration.


### Code snippets to reproduce the issue

### Attack pattern observed in production
Bots perform the following sequence:

GET /login → obtain valid Laravel session + CSRF token
POST /livewire-{hash}/update with a well-formed snapshot from a Filament installation (e.g. filament.pages.auth.login, filament.livewire.database-notifications) → HTTP 500
Subsequent requests → HTTP 419 (CSRF token invalidated after first request)

The bot recycles snapshots with valid checksums from other Filament installations. Since the APP_KEY is stable across deployments, the checksum remains valid indefinitely.

### Screenshots/screen recordings

### Stack Trace
```
[2026-06-11 02:44:39] production.ERROR: Unable to find component: [filament.pages.auth.login]
Livewire\Exceptions\ComponentNotFoundException(code: 0):
Unable to find component: [filament.pages.auth.login]
at vendor/livewire/livewire/src/Factory/Factory.php:76

#0 vendor/livewire/livewire/src/Factory/Factory.php(88): Livewire\Factory\Factory->resolveComponentNameAndClass()
#1 vendor/livewire/livewire/src/Features/SupportReleaseTokens/ReleaseToken.php(15): Livewire\Factory\Factory->resolveComponentClass()
#2 vendor/livewire/livewire/src/Features/SupportReleaseTokens/SupportReleaseTokens.php(20): Livewire\Features\SupportReleaseTokens\ReleaseToken::verify()
#3 vendor/livewire/livewire/src/EventBus.php(87): Livewire\Features\SupportReleaseTokens\SupportReleaseTokens::Livewire\Features\SupportReleaseTokens\{closure}()
#4 vendor/livewire/livewire/src/helpers.php(98): Livewire\EventBus->trigger()
#5 vendor/livewire/livewire/src/Mechanisms/HandleComponents/Checksum.php(23): Livewire\trigger()
#6 vendor/livewire/livewire/src/Mechanisms/HandleComponents/HandleComponents.php(264): Livewire\Mechanisms\HandleComponents\Checksum::verify()
#7 vendor/livewire/livewire/src/Mechanisms/HandleComponents/HandleComponents.php(230): Livewire\Mechanisms\HandleComponents\HandleComponents->fromSnapshot()
#8 vendor/livewire/livewire/src/LivewireManager.php(131): Livewire\Mechanisms\HandleComponents\HandleComponents->update()
``` 

### How do you expect it to work?

A snapshot referencing a non-existent component should be treated as a corrupt/invalid payload and return HTTP 400, consistent with how other invalid payloads are handled since the security hardening in v4.2.0.

### Please confirm (incomplete submissions will not be addressed)

- [x] I have provided easy and step-by-step instructions to reproduce the bug.
- [x] I have provided code samples as text and NOT images.
- [x] I understand my bug report will be removed if I haven't met the criteria above.

title: Methods executed behind listener don't trigger `wire:loading` even when listed in `wire:target`
author: MaxencePaulin
body:
### Livewire version v4.3.1 ### Laravel version v13.13.0 ### PHP version 8.4.14 ### Browser and operating system Brave on Windows ### Describe the issue you're experiencing When a method is triggered via a listener, `wire:loading` with `wire:target` does not activate even when the method is explicitly listed in `wire:target`. This makes it impossible to scope loading indicators to specific actions when multiple are present on the same page and one of them can be triggered by an event. A workaround might be to set up a listener via an Alpine component inside the Livewire component using `$wire.$on` and `$wire.$call('action')`, but I think this could be handled automatically by Livewire. In my case, I didn't want to use a JavaScript listener for maintainability reasons. I have multiple actions on the same page, and the only way to scope `wire:loading` directives is through an explicit `wire:target`. Having to abandon the PHP listener in favor of a JavaScript one simply to isolate wire:loading states, especially when a component is starting to grow large and handle multiple actions, can quickly make that component harder to maintain, and leads you to wonder why you're not just using Livewire's built-in PHP features. ### Code snippets to reproduce the issue >This example is with a child, but the real use case has a generic component, not always used as a "child" of the listener component. `$parent.setData()` is not a solution in my case. 1. Trigger a list refresh via the item modal component's button 2. Observe that no loading indicator appears 3. Trigger a list refresh via the "Refresh list itself" button 4. Observe that the loading indicator appears Here is a live Wirebox to reproduce it : https://wirebox.app/b/xe36g You can see directly the code snippets used below : ```php // List Component
List Component
Loading...
BLADE; } #[On('refresh-list')] public function setData(): void { sleep(2); } } ``` ```php // ItemModal Component

Item Modal

BLADE; } public function action(): void { // } } ``` ### Screenshots/screen recordings // ### How do you expect it to work? When a method is called via a listener, `wire:target` should scope the loading indicator the same way it does when the method is called directly from the component. Note that without `wire:target`, calling the same method via a listener does trigger `wire:loading`, the issue is isolated to scoping with `wire:target`. I found an old discussion (2020) about this with no resolution : https://github.com/livewire/livewire/discussions/3080 And an old closed PR here : https://github.com/livewire/livewire/pull/8269 ### Please confirm (incomplete submissions will not be addressed) - [x] I have provided easy and step-by-step instructions to reproduce the bug. - [x] I have provided code samples as text and NOT images. - [x] I understand my bug report will be removed if I haven't met the criteria above.

title: `x-bind:disabled` binding overridden after renderless action when combined with `wire:loading.attr="disabled"`
author: MaxencePaulin
body:
### Livewire version v4.3.1 (v3 too) ### Laravel version v13.13.0 ### PHP version 8.4.14 ### Browser and operating system Brave on Windows ### Describe the issue you're experiencing When a button has both `wire:loading.attr="disabled"` and `x-bind:disabled` bound to a condition or property, triggering a #[Renderless] action from the component causes the `x-bind:disabled` binding to be overridden and stop working correctly. In this example, `x-bind:disabled` should permanently evaluate to true, but after a renderless action is called, the button becomes enabled. ### Code snippets to reproduce the issue 1. Observe that the "Some action" button is disabled 2. Click "Renderless action" to trigger a renderless action 3. Observe that the "Some action" button is no longer disabled Here is a live Wirebox to reproduce it : https://wirebox.app/b/xg1g6 You can see directly the code snippets used below : ```php
BLADE; } public function someAction(): void { // } #[Renderless] public function renderlessAction(): void { // } } ``` ### Screenshots/screen recordings // ### How do you expect it to work? The x-bind:disabled binding should be work, even when a renderless action is called. > [!IMPORTANT] >Note that the issue does not occur when either removing the #[Renderless] attribute from the action, or adding wire:click to the button that has `wire:loading.attr="disabled"`. ### Please confirm (incomplete submissions will not be addressed) - [x] I have provided easy and step-by-step instructions to reproduce the bug. - [x] I have provided code samples as text and NOT images. - [x] I understand my bug report will be removed if I haven't met the criteria above.

title: @click (Alpine x-on:click) not initialized on fresh page load. Works after wire:navigate
author: harhoo
body:
### Livewire version 4.2.4 ### Laravel version 12.56 ### PHP version 8.3 ### Browser and operating system Chrome on MacOS ### Describe the issue you're experiencing @click on a plain {{-- Method 2: onclick (native HTML) — WORKS on fresh page load --}} {{-- wire:navigate to self — triggers the re-initialisation that "fixes" @click --}} wire:navigate (reload)

Steps to reproduce:

  1. Hard-refresh this page
  2. Click "Alpine @click" in the header → counter does NOT increment
  3. Click "Native onclick" in the header → counter increments ✓
  4. Click "wire:navigate and back" — reloads this page via Livewire SPA navigation
  5. Click "Alpine @click" → counter NOW increments ✓

Alpine @click clicks

0

broken on fresh page load

Native onclick clicks

0

always works

Expected behaviour:

Both buttons should increment their counter on every click, regardless of whether the page was hard-refreshed or navigated to via wire:navigate.

Actual behaviour:

The @click (Alpine x-on:click) directive has no effect on a fresh page load. After a wire:navigate navigation, it works. Native onclick works in both cases.
